target.name: NVision Imaging Technologies
target.founder: Martin Plenio, Ilai Schwartz, Fedor Jelezko, Alex Retzker

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
NVision Imaging Technologies, a Series A biotech company founded by Plenio, Schwartz, Jelezko, and Retzker, has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B), and Neko Health ($1.8B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $40M off the $20M raised proxy. Peer-Median anchors to the valuation of 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which show a median of $810M, with a range from $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp anchoring (P/S median of 6.1x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied without the target's revenue, which is not disclosed.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $34M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $425M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty in early-stage biotech valuation: the peer set itself spans nearly two orders of magnitude, and stage heterogeneity within adjacent rounds creates material ambiguity. Stage-Progression yields a conservative floor; Peer-Median dominates but is sensitive to outlier valuations in the comp set. Revenue generation and clinical/technical validation progress are critical unknowns narrowing this range.
